section_name: Operation of Vehicle in Violation of Chapter; Offense
chapter_title: Inscription Required on State, Mun., & Cnty. Motor Vehicles
text:
(a) A person commits an offense if the person:

(1) operates on a municipal street or on a highway a motor vehicle or piece of equipment that does not have the inscription required by this chapter; or

(2) uses a motor vehicle that is exempt by rule under Section 721.003 (Exemption from Inscription Requirement for Certain State-owned Motor Vehicles) , and that use is not expressly specified by the rule.

(b) An offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $25 or more than $100.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 721. Inscription Required on State, Mun., & Cnty. Motor Vehicles
##### § 721.006 — Operation of Vehicle in Violation of Chapter; Offense

(a) A person commits an offense if the person:

    (1) operates on a municipal street or on a highway a motor vehicle or piece of equipment that does not have the inscription required by this chapter; or

    (2) uses a motor vehicle that is exempt by rule under Section 721.003 (Exemption from Inscription Requirement for Certain State-owned Motor Vehicles) , and that use is not expressly specified by the rule.

(b) An offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $25 or more than $100.

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: A person commits an offense if the person:
operates on a municipal street or on a highway a motor vehicle or piece of equipment that does not have the inscription required by this chapter; or

uses a motor vehicle that is exempt by rule under Section 721.003 (Exemption from Inscription Requirement for Certain State-owned Motor Vehicles) , and that use is not expressly specified by the rule.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: An offense under this section is a misdemeanor punishable by a fine of not less than $25 or more than $100.

history.raw: Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995.
